codeReview.summary: The feature adds coverage asserting blocked tasks stay visible in warcraft_status and updates the blocked-resume seam expectation, but it does not add any doctor-surface verification even though the feature contract explicitly requires status and doctor to remain non-contradictory for blocked tasks.

issues.description: The new regression coverage only exercises `warcraft_status`; the feature's expected behavior also requires doctor/status parity for blocked tasks, but this feature neither changes doctor behavior nor adds a doctor-tool assertion proving blocked tasks remain coherently represented there. Because the targeted seam command can skip entirely, the implemented evidence does not fully cover the contract that blocked status and doctor surfaces remain non-contradictory.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `audit-fix-worker` skill tells workers to run the requested validators, but it does not explicitly call out that some mission E2E seam commands may exit 0 while skipping all assertions due to host-lane preflight. This feature's handoff had to explain that nuance manually.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff 2026-03-11T08-22-20-598Z__scrutiny-blocked-status-contract-fix__fd8a2d59-79af-4334-9892-b27452c03f2c.json notes `bun test .../blocked-resume-seam.test.ts` exited 0 with both host-lane tests skipped; .factory/skills/audit-fix-worker/SKILL.md step 6/9 does not mention recording skip-only validators separately.

summary: Fail: the commit cleanly scopes blocked-status coverage and updates the seam expectation, but it does not supply evidence for the required doctor/status parity branch, so the feature does not fully satisfy its expected behavior.
